statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

statistics.description: The number of times a player loses possession to the other team.

statistics.description: The number of times when the defense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot by the offense.

articles.headline: A'ja Wilson listed as questionable for Commissioner's Cup final
articles.description: Four-time WNBA MVP A'ja Wilson is listed as questionable because of a right leg injury ahead of the Commissioner's Cup championship game against Liberty.

images.name: A'ja Wilson [1024x576]
images.caption: A'ja Wilson is listed as questionable ahead of the Aces' Commissioner Cup championship matchup against the New York Liberty.

articles.headline: Mercury rookie Jovana Nogić to miss rest of 2026 season
articles.description: Mercury rookie Jovana Nogić will miss the remainder of the 2026 season because of personal reasons, the team announced Monday.

images.name: Jovana Nogic [600x400]
images.caption: Phoenix guard Jovana Nogic, a 28-year-old rookie, is averaging 14.0 points on 52.3% shooting on 3-pointers.

articles.headline: Fantasy women's hoops: Nelson-Ododa, Leite among this week's top player picks
articles.description: Here are four free agents to add to your fantasy women's basketball lineups this week.

images.name: Olivia Nelson-Ododa [1296x729]
images.caption: Olivia Nelson-Ododa of the Connecticut Sun is available in 82.7% of ESPN leagues.

images.name: Cecilia Zandalasini [1296x729]
images.caption: Cecilia Zandalasini has scored in double figures in each of her past three games.

images.name: Kelsey Plum [1296x729]
images.caption: Kelsey Plum is expected to be out for at least four weeks with a left leg injury.

images.name: Sonia Citron [1296x729]
images.caption: Sonia Citron of the Washington Mystics has scored in double-figures in eight straight games.

images.name: Carla Leite nails the trey
images.caption: Carla Leite nails the trey

articles.headline: 2026 WNBA championship odds: Lynx continue to see odds shorten; now standalone favorites
articles.description: The Lynx move ahead of the Aces and Liberty as favorites to win the WNBA championship.

images.name: Olivia Miles [1296x729]
images.caption: Olivia Miles and the Minnesota Lynx are now alone in front as favorites to win the WNBA championship.
images.alt: Minnesota Lynx guard Olivia Miles (5) dribbles past Dallas Wings guard Azzi Fudd (35) during the WNBA game between the Dallas Wings and the Minnesota Lynx on June 28, 2026, at College Park Center in Arlington, TX.

images.name: Natasha Howard of Minnesota Lynx [608x342]
images.caption: A strong start to the season has the Lynx tied with the Aces for the shortest title odds, just ahead of the Liberty.

images.name: Olivia Miles [1296x729]
images.caption: The Lynx have seen their odds shorten but have a tough schedule this week.
images.alt: Minnesota Lynx guard Olivia Miles (5) drives past Dallas Wings guard Paige Bueckers during the second half of a WNBA basketball game in Arlington, Texas, Thursday, May 14, 2026.

images.name: Olivia Miles [1296x729]
images.caption: Rookie of the Year favorite Olivia Miles is helping drive the early success of the Minnesota Lynx.

images.name: Paige Bueckers [1296x729]
images.caption: Paige Bueckers and her 19.3 PPG is a big reason for the encouraging start by the Wings.
images.alt: Dallas Wings guard Paige Bueckers (5) drives to the basket against Chicago Sky guard Natasha Cloud (9) during the second half at Wintrust Arena.

images.name: A'ja Wilson [1296x729]
images.caption: The Las Vegas Aces, led by reigning MVP A'ja Wilson, are again one of the favorites to win the WNBA championship this season.
images.alt: Las Vegas Aces center A'ja Wilson (22) celebrates a play with teammates during the first half of a WNBA preseason basketball game against the Dallas Wings in Austin, Texas, Sunday, May 3, 2026.

images.name: Satou Sabally [1296x729]
images.caption: The New York Liberty made some free agent moves, most notably signing Satou Sabally.

images.name: Angel Reese [1296x729]
images.caption: Angel Reese heads to Atlanta after two seasons in Chicago.
images.alt: Chicago Sky forward Angel Reese (5) dribbles against the Atlanta Dream in the first quarter at Gateway Center Arena at College Park.

articles.headline: Liberty's Marine Johannes is signing a multiyear Unrivaled deal
articles.description: New York Liberty guard Marine Johannes is signing a multiyear deal with Unrivaled.

images.name: Marine Johannes [600x400]
images.caption: Marine Johannes is signing a multi-year deal with Unrivaled.

articles.headline: WNBA Commissioner's Cup 2026: Keys to Aces-Liberty final
articles.description: New York hosts Las Vegas on Tuesday in a clash between the WNBA's winningest teams over the past three seasons.

images.name: A'ja Wilson, Breanna Stewart [1296x729]
images.caption: The winners of the past six WNBA MVP awards -- A'ja Wilson (2020, '22, '24 and '25), Breanna Stewart (2023) and Jonquel Jones (2021) -- will be on court in Tuesday's Commissioner's Cup final.

images.name: Jackie Young and Pauline Astier [1296x729]
images.caption: New York rookie Pauline Astier is averaging 10.1 points and 3.8 assists this season and had seven points, two assists and no turnovers in 20 minutes in a win over Las Vegas and Jackie Young on June 23.

images.name: NaLyssa Smith [1296x729]
images.caption: NaLyssa Smith is having her best season statistically in three years, but her numbers were subpar in a loss to New York on June 23.

images.name: Las Vegas Aces vs. New York Liberty - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Las Vegas Aces vs. New York Liberty, 06/23/2026

articles.byline: Kareem Copeland and Kendra Andrews and Michael Voepel

articles.headline: Las Vegas visits New York following Wilson's 30-point game
articles.description: Las Vegas Aces (14-5, 10-4 Western Conference) at New York Liberty (12-8, 8-1 Eastern Conference)

categories.description: Las Vegas Aces @ New York Liberty

event.description: Las Vegas Aces @ New York Liberty

articles.headline: Scuffling Liberty call for more 'heart' after latest loss
articles.description: The Liberty are searching for answers after their fourth loss in five games, with star forward Breanna Stewart challenging her team to play with more heart.

images.name: Sabrina Ionescu knocks down the 3
images.caption: Sabrina Ionescu nails the trey

articles.headline: Charles ties season high with 13 points as Valkyries down Liberty 76-67
articles.description: — Kaila Charles tied a season high with 13 points off the bench and the Golden State Valkyries held the New York Liberty to their lowest-scoring first half of the season en route to a 76-67 victory on Sunday night.

images.name: Golden State Valkyries vs. New York Liberty - Game Highlights

articles.headline: A'ja Wilson, Las Vegas Aces top Kamilla Cardoso, Chicago Sky
articles.description: Four-time MVP A'ja Wilson finished with 30 points and 15 rebounds Sunday as the Aces posted a 107-99 victory over Kamilla Cardoso and the Chicago Sky at United Center, Las Vegas' 14th win of the season and second in a row.

images.name: A'ja Wilson scores 30 vs. Chicago Sky
images.caption: A'ja Wilson scores 30 vs. Chicago Sky

articles.headline: Mystics win marathon matchup, beat Fire in record-tying 4 OTs
articles.description: Sonia Citron scored a career-high 32 points, including the basket that put her team ahead to stay with 21.4 seconds left, and the Washington Mystics held off the Portland Fire 124-123 on Sunday in four overtimes -- tying the longest game in WNBA history.

images.name: Michaela Onyenwere [600x400]
images.caption: Michaela Onyenwere, who scored a career-high 30 points, was among eight players who played 45-plus minutes Sunday, the most in a game in WNBA history.

images.name: Carla Leite can't get it to fall as Fire lose in 4OT to Mystics
images.caption: Carla Leite can't get it to fall as Fire lose in 4OT to Mystics

articles.headline: Mystics win a marathon matchup, topping Fire 124-123 in record-tying 4 OTs
articles.description: — Sonia Citron scored 32 points, including the basket that put her team ahead to stay with 21.4 seconds left, and the Washington Mystics held off the Portland Fire 124-123 on Sunday in four overtimes — tying the longest game in WNBA history.

articles.headline: A’ja Wilson has a WNBA first with 30 points, 15 rebounds, 4 steals, 3 blocks in Aces' win over Sky
articles.description: — A’ja Wilson had 30 points, 15 rebounds, four steals and three blocks to become the first player in WNBA history to reach those marks in a game as the Las Vegas Aces beat the Chicago Sky 107-99 on Sunday.

images.name: A'ja Wilson scores 30 vs. Chicago Sky
images.caption: A'ja Wilson scores 30 vs. Chicago Sky

article.story: CHICAGO -- — Angel Reese had 17 points and 17 rebounds in the first regular-season matchup against her former team, Rhyne Howard also scored 17 points, and the Atlanta Dream beat the Chicago Sky 82-75 on Tuesday night. Reese, who returned to Chicago where she was drafted No. 7 overall in 2024, secured her eighth double-double of the season with six minutes left in the third quarter. Her 57 career double-doubles are the most through 75 games in WNBA history. Howard eclipsed 2,500 career points and became the youngest player in WNBA history to record 2,500 points, 500 rebounds, 500 assists, 200 steals and 100 blocks. Naz Hillmon finished with 16 points and Allisha Gray, who was held to two points in the first half, added 14 for Atlanta (8-3). Jordin Canada also scored 14 points. Natasha Cloud scored 18 points off the bench for Chicago (4-8). Skylar Diggins added 17 points and Kamilla Cardoso had 13. Cloud sank a long jumper with 3.5 seconds left in the third to give Chicago a 58-57 lead. She also began the fourth quarter scoring with a 3-pointer for a four-point lead. Hillmon made a layup, off a nice pass from Reese, to give Atlanta a 73-70 lead and she added a 3-pointer on the next possession for a six-point advantage. Reese made it 80-70 with 56.1 left following a 9-0 run. Up next Atlanta returns home to play New York on Thursday. Chicago plays at Indiana on Thursday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
